steps.title: Set up a substitution to apply the functional equation
steps.content: We're given $f(3x) = x - 6$ and need to find $f(6)$. Since the left side of the equation has input $3x$, we need to find the value of $x$ that makes $3x = 6$ so we can evaluate the function at that point. Solving $3x = 6$ gives $x = 2$.

steps.title: Substitute and evaluate
steps.content: Substituting $x = 2$ into the functional equation $f(3x) = x - 6$: $$f(6) = f(3 \cdot 2) = 2 - 6 = -4$$ That matches Choice B — $-4$.
